name: South China Sea
climate: Tropical maritime; hot and humid; monsoon-driven; typhoon-prone (June-November); heavy rainfall

neighbors.name: ASEAN
neighbors.relevance: Multiple ASEAN members (VNM, PHL, MYS, BRN, IDN) are claimant states in SCS disputes

neighbors.name: Strait of Malacca
neighbors.relevance: Western gateway to the SCS; Malacca traffic feeds directly into SCS shipping lanes

neighbors.name: Taiwan Strait
neighbors.relevance: Northern exit of the SCS; Taiwan controls Pratas Islands; linked military escalation scenarios

neighbors.name: Indo-Pacific
neighbors.relevance: SCS is the central flashpoint of Indo-Pacific strategic competition

neighbors.name: Pearl River Delta
neighbors.relevance: PRD ports are the largest origin/destination for SCS shipping traffic

neighbors.name: Greater Bay Area
neighbors.relevance: GBA's economic output depends on unimpeded SCS maritime access

links.description: ASEAN claimant states contest China's nine-dash line; ASEAN unity strained by competing claims
Malacca is the western chokepoint feeding into SCS; disruption in either affects the other
Northern SCS connects to Taiwan Strait; combined blockade scenarios threaten global shipping
Quad nations conduct freedom of navigation operations in the SCS
AUKUS submarine capability is partly aimed at maintaining SCS access and deterrence
PRD manufacturing exports are routed through SCS; disruption would halt global electronics supply
SCS is the most contested maritime space within the broader Indo-Pacific framework
Maritime Silk Road routes pass through the SCS connecting China to Southeast Asia and beyond
